title: Fluentd Worker Process Crash and Supervisor Restart Detection (multi-worker blast radius, segfault and OOM classification, supervisor respawn storms, Fluent Bit kube correlation, mstats anchors, audit execve option)

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode grouping on shipper entities by cluster and owner_team

dataSources: (index=fluent OR index=platform OR index=obs OR index=kubernetes OR index=os OR index=linux OR index=audit) sourcetype=fluentd:log OR sourcetype=fluent:logs OR sourcetype=fluentd:supervisor for supervisor respawn worker died SIGSEGV OOM unexpected error without-source hints; journald or syslog with td-agent.service Main process exited; sourcetype=kube:events for fluentd fluent-bit pods with OOMKilled Killed BackOff; metrics index mstats cpu.utilization mem.utilization host anchors; optional sourcetype=linux:audit OR auditd execve for td-agent fluent-bit; lookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv; cmdb puppet_facts for environment enrichment
app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud + Splunk Connect for Kubernetes (optional) + Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector (optional sidecar metrics)
description: Correlates Fluentd supervisor and worker crash signatures, systemd journal exits for td-agent, Kubernetes OOMKilled and BackOff events for shipper pods, host CPU and memory metrics via mstats, and optional Linux audit execve context to classify segfault versus Ruby panic versus OOM, detect fast-loop respawn storms above roughly three restarts per minute, and expose estimated log gap seconds with routing guidance when supervisor flags reduce config dump verbosity.
value: Reliability leaders gain a defensible timeline when log volume looks healthy yet history has holes: crash-first fusion separates one-off operator reloads from pathological respawn loops, distinguishes kernel OOM pressure from native plugin faults, and gives incident commanders enrichment that ties nodes and pods to owning teams before executives ask whether observability itself failed.
grandmaExplanation: Picture a string of relay runners passing a baton: if runners keep tripping and a coach keeps swapping people in faster than the race can continue, long stretches of the track never get recorded. We watch for that frantic substitution rhythm so crews can steady the team before silent gaps appear in what the fleet thought it logged.
implementation: Land fluentd:log, fluent:logs, fluentd:supervisor, journald td-agent lines, kube:events for shipper pods, mstats CPU and memory anchors, optional auditd execve; normalize host keys; schedule observability_uc_13_7_4_fluent_supervisor_crash_storm every five to ten minutes with earliest=-4h@h; join fluentd_node_inventory.csv and puppet_facts for environment; route critical and high severities to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with the observability pipeline squad that runs Fluentd on virtual machines, Fluent Bit on Kubernetes, Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector sidecars, and any Treasure Data style td-agent packages still in long-term support. This use case is intentionally narrow: it chases process death and supervisor-driven resurrection, not buffer queue depth, not downstream HEC saturation, and not generic disk alarms. Multi-worker Fluentd deployments under a Ruby supervisor fork several children; when one child dies from a native plugin fault, the parent may respawn quickly enough that operators only notice holes in log continuity hours later. Fluent Bit uses a different supervision model but still surfaces OOM kills through the container runtime and still benefits from correlated host metrics. Supervisor flags such as without-source and suppress-config-dump change how much context lands in local logs; you must not assume every crash prints a full Ruby stack when operators intentionally silence config dumps during incidents.

Start from a clear inventory stance. Maintain fluentd_node_inventory.csv keyed by host or node with cluster, owner_team, environment, optional region, and optional workload_class so alert rows route to the correct squad without ad hoc spreadsheet joins. Where Kubernetes owns the shipper, carry node name and DaemonSet name in the inventory or derive them through stable labels at ingest time. Splunk Connect for Kubernetes and the Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector often cohabit the same nodes; stamp a shipper_family field at collection time so analysts can tell a td-agent line from an otelcol line before they open an incident bridge.

Data plane choices matter for fidelity. A dedicated TA or scripted input that tails td-agent logs into sourcetype=fluentd:log preserves LINE_BREAKER control for multiline Ruby traces. When you rely only on container stdout, the CRI wrapper may split stack traces across events unless you configure a multiline parser upstream of Splunk. For systemd hosts, forwarding journald with fields like _SYSTEMD_UNIT, _PID, and MESSAGE intact is non-negotiable; stripping structured journal keys makes it impossible to distinguish Main process exited, code=exited, status=1 from a clean supervisor reload.

Parsing posture: add transforms that normalize supervisor phrases such as unexpected error, worker 0 died with signal SIGSEGV, restarting worker, and suppress-config-dump hints into a stable signal_class token at index time when feasible, but keep raw _raw available for forensics. Props and transforms should not truncate at eight kilobytes when native plugins dump jemalloc stacks. TIME_PREFIX and TIME_FORMAT must honor subsecond timestamps on fast respawn loops or streamstats windows will mis-order events.

RBAC and secrets: collectors read kube-apiserver event streams and node journals; service accounts need least privilege and rotation telemetry. TLS material for outputs must be tracked beside crash timelines because secret rotation races produce exit storms that look like code regressions. Legal reviewers should confirm whether crash excerpts may contain customer payloads; redact in pager templates while retaining full raw access for a break-glass role.

Scope boundaries: UC-13.7.1 remains the buffer saturation and retry exhaustion narrative with monitor_agent and Fluent Bit storage metrics. This document is the crash-first companion. UC-13.2.x style ITSI rollups may consume outputs from this search but do not replace the multisource fusion prescribed here. When both fire, treat buffer UC as backlog storytelling and this UC as stability storytelling until timelines prove a single root cause.

Risk briefing: deliberate rolling upgrades of DaemonSets create benign restart bursts; require dwell and change_ticket correlation before customer pages. Chaos experiments that kill fluent-bit pods will trip kube events arms on purpose; label drills so governance teams expect the noise. Ruby garbage collection stop-the-world pauses can trip watchdogs that look like native crashes unless you capture JFR or extended GC logs in adjacent tooling.

Differentiation recap: worker death, supervisor respawn, signal classification, kube BackOff and OOMKilled correlation, host CPU and memory anchors via mstats, optional audit execve context for supervisor binaries, fast-loop storm detection above three restarts per minute, and explicit handling of supervisor flags that reduce log verbosity.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms plus supporting lookups before you schedule the saved search.

Arm A, engine and supervisor text: land Fluentd fluentd.log or container stdout as sourcetype=fluentd:log or sourcetype=fluent:logs when your mapping follows the community convention. Capture supervisor lines that mention respawn, worker died, SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, unexpected error, plugin load failures, and without-source. If operators run fluentd:supervisor as a distinct sourcetype for stderr split, include it in the same arm. Configure LINE_BREAKER and SHOULD_LINEMERGE so Ruby stacks stay intact. TRUNCATE should remain high enough for native plugin faults.

Arm B, systemd journal: forward journald with _SYSTEMD_UNIT=td-agent.service or fluentd.service into index=os or index=platform. Preserve MESSAGE, CODE, and PRIORITY. Map rsyslog equivalents only when journal metadata is absent, and document the weaker fidelity. Test that Main process exited lines appear within one collection interval of a manual systemctl restart in lab.

Arm C, Kubernetes events: stream kube:events for fluentd, fluent-bit, and td-agent pods. Retain reason, message, involvedObject.name, and source.host when available. NetworkPolicy for the collector must allow watches against the API server you expect in each cluster context. For multi-cluster fleets, normalize cluster name at ingest so the same lookup keys work everywhere.

Arm D, host metrics via mstats: ingest process or host utilization metrics into a metrics index such as metrics or a dedicated infra_metrics index. Prefer explicit metric names your organization already accelerates; map cpu.utilization and mem.utilization or equivalent counters for nodes running shippers. If you only have Splunk Observability Cloud exports, translate into a compatible metric_name before this search runs. Document which cardinality reduction you applied so Job Inspector stays predictable.

Arm E, optional Linux audit: when security approves auditd forwarding, capture execve lines for td-agent, fluentd, and fluent-bit binaries. This arm helps distinguish legitimate supervisor restarts from unexpected binary relaunches during supply-chain investigations. If audit volume is too high, sample with a dedicated input that filters on key paths such as /opt/td-agent and /usr/local/bin/fluent-bit.

Lookup maintenance: refresh fluentd_node_inventory.csv weekly or automate KV store replication from CMDB. Include IPv6 management addresses only when host keys match the telemetry you emit. For Kubernetes-only shippers, you may key inventory on node name and join upward to cluster.

Props.conf guidance: strip ANSI color for container collectors, preserve UTC offsets, and avoid aggressive SEDCMD that removes signal numbers. Tags for CIM: map process start and stop narratives into Endpoint where your data model program requires it, map configuration reload events into Change when you emit structured reload success flags, and map Performance only after you validate metric_name mapping for mstats arms.

Pre-save validation: run each arm standalone for earliest=-1h and confirm non-zero volume on a canary host. Induce a controlled fluent-bit OOMKill in lab and verify kube and engine arms both fire. Validate mstats returns rows for the same host keys you use in text arms.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
journalctl -u td-agent.service -n 200 --no-pager
kubectl -n logging get events --field-selector involvedObject.kind=Pod | rg -i fluent
/opt/td-agent/bin/fluentd --version
/opt/td-agent/bin/ruby -v
```

Dashboards: host a supervisor timeline panel, a kube event strip chart, and an mstats overlay for CPU and memory on the node. Color rows by severity from this search and drill into raw _raw for stack traces.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_4_fluent_supervisor_crash_storm with a five-minute schedule in dense fleets and a ten-minute schedule when API rate limits constrain kube event collection, using dispatch earliest=-4h@h and latest=now. Page when severity is critical, warn when high, and route medium to platform chat with owner_team in the title. Throttle duplicate host and pod_name pairs for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager text so on-call engineers open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans engine logs, systemd journals, Kubernetes events, mstats anchors, and optional audit execve context so one silent lane does not hide a genuine death spiral. bin span=1m collapses noise before streamstats measures five-minute restart sums. eventstats adds cluster_restart_avg to distinguish fleet-wide upgrade ripples from isolated bad nodes. case maps restart_per_min, oom_count, and signal_class hints into critical, high, medium, and low tiers consistent with platform policy. coalesce merges missing pod_name, node, and cluster stand-in values introduced by VM-only arms. inputlookup enriches owner context from fluentd_node_inventory.csv. join adds puppet_environment from cmdb when teams still rely on Puppet facts for lifecycle labels.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.4 Fluentd worker crash supervisor restart storm; earliest=-4h multisearch fluent_engine systemd kube mstats audit; tune restart_per_min thresholds per fleet")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=platform OR index=obs OR index=kubernetes) (sourcetype=fluentd:log OR sourcetype="fluent:logs" OR sourcetype=fluentd:supervisor) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"restart|respawn|restarting|terminated|worker.*died|killed|sigsegv|sigabrt|segmentation|oom|out of memory|unexpected error|fatal|plugin|cannot allocate|without-source|suppress-config")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,splunk_server,""))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes.pod_name,pod,pod_name,k8s_pod,""))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes.node_name,node_name,node,k8s_node,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval restart_evt=if(match(lr,"restart|respawn|starting worker|supervisor|spawn|detached|without-source"),1,0)
      | eval oom_evt=if(match(lr,"oom|out of memory|oomkilled|cannot allocate memory|killed process"),1,0)
      | eval signal_class=case(oom_evt==1,"oom_pressure", match(lr,"sigsegv|segmentation|signal 11"),"native_segv", match(lr,"sigabrt|abort|signal 6"),"native_abort", match(lr,"unexpected error|fatal error|error class"),"ruby_panic", match(lr,"plugin|stack"),"plugin_fault", true(),"supervisor_recycle")
      | eval lane="fluent_supervisor_log"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ search (index=os OR index=platform OR index=linux OR index=fluent) (sourcetype=journald OR sourcetype=syslog OR sourcetype=linux_messages_syslog) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (td-agent OR fluentd OR "td-agent.service" OR fluent-bit OR fluentd.service)
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"main process exited|entered failed state|respawn|supervisor|td-agent|fluen|code=killed|oom|status=1")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,""))))
      | eval pod_name=""
      | eval node=""
      | eval cluster="undetermined"
      | eval restart_evt=if(match(lr,"exited|respawn|failed state|start request repeated|will restart"),1,0)
      | eval oom_evt=if(match(lr,"oom|code=killed|out of memory"),1,0)
      | eval signal_class=case(oom_evt==1,"oom_systemd", match(lr,"segv|signal 11|abrt"),"signal_exit", true(),"systemd_lifecycle")
      | eval lane="systemd_unit_journal"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ search (index=kubernetes OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=kube:events earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval inv=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name,involved_object_name,name,""))))
      | where match(inv,"fluentd|fluent-bit|fluentbit|td-agent")
      | eval reason=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(reason,Reason,""))))
      | where match(reason,"oomkilled|killed|backoff|failed|unhealthy|error")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(source_host,host,"kube_apiserver"))))
      | eval pod_name=inv
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(source,node_name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval restart_evt=if(match(reason,"backoff|failed|killed|unhealthy"),1,0)
      | eval oom_evt=if(match(reason,"oom"),1,0)
      | eval signal_class=case(oom_evt==1,"kube_oomkill", match(reason,"backoff"),"kube_backoff", true(),"kube_pod_storm")
      | eval lane="kube_shipper_events"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ | mstats prestats=t avg(_value) AS cpu_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="cpu.utilization" span=5m BY host
      | join type=left max=0 host [ | mstats prestats=t avg(_value) AS rss_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="mem.utilization" span=5m BY host ]
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval pod_name=""
      | eval node=""
      | eval cluster="undetermined"
      | eval restart_evt=0
      | eval oom_evt=0
      | eval signal_class="perf_host_anchor"
      | eval lane="mstats_shipper_host"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=round(coalesce(cpu_v,0),2)
      | eval p95_rss_mb=round(coalesce(rss_v,0)*10,2)
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ search (index=linux OR index=os OR index=audit) (sourcetype=linux:audit OR sourcetype=auditd) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (fluentd OR "td-agent" OR fluent-bit OR "/opt/td-agent")
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"execve|type=execve|exe=.*fluent|td-agent")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,""))))
      | eval pod_name=""
      | eval node=""
      | eval cluster="undetermined"
      | eval restart_evt=0
      | eval oom_evt=0
      | eval signal_class="audit_supervisor_exec"
      | eval lane="linux_audit_execve"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
| eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,""))))
| eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,""))))
| eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,"undetermined"))))
| fillnull value=0 restart_evt oom_evt p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(restart_evt) AS restart_min sum(oom_evt) AS oom_min values(signal_class) AS sig_vals max(p95_cpu_pct) AS p95_cpu_pct max(p95_rss_mb) AS p95_rss_mb BY _time host pod_name node cluster
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(restart_min) AS restart_count sum(oom_min) AS oom_count BY host pod_name
| eval restart_per_min=round(restart_count/5,3)
| eventstats avg(restart_count) AS cluster_restart_avg BY cluster
| eval severity=case(restart_per_min>=3 OR oom_count>=1 OR match(mvjoin(sig_vals,""),"oom"),"critical", restart_per_min>=1 AND restart_per_min<3,"high", restart_count>=3 AND cluster_restart_avg>=2,"high", restart_count>=1 AND restart_count<6,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval signal_class=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(sig_vals),0),"mixed")
| eval dropped_log_seconds_estimate=round(restart_count*15,0)
| eval recommended_action=case(match(signal_class,"oom"),"raise_memory_limits_review_cgroups_and_jemalloc_tune_collect_kernel_oom_trace", match(signal_class,"native_segv|native_abort|plugin_fault"),"pin_plugin_versions_core_dump_native_stanza_offending_output", match(signal_class,"ruby_panic"),"capture_ruby_backtrace_fix_filter_regex_or_gem_conflict", match(signal_class,"kube_backoff"),"kubectl_describe_events_check_probes_and_image_digest", match(signal_class,"systemd_lifecycle"),"journalctl_td_agent_status_validate_unit_and_disk", true(),"correlate_supervisor_flags_and_multiline_stack_traces")
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,shipper_host,""))))
     | fields host cluster owner_team environment ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=puppet_facts earliest=-24h latest=now
      | dedup host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | rename environment AS puppet_environment
      | table host puppet_environment ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,puppet_environment,"unknown")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| table _time host pod_name node cluster environment owner_team restart_count oom_count signal_class p95_rss_mb p95_cpu_pct severity dropped_log_seconds_estimate recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_4_fluent_supervisor_crash_storm_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_4_fluent_supervisor_crash_storm | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if multisearch queue time exceeds your internal objective, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on host and pod_name, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap kube event cardinality with dedup rules only after security agrees you are not dropping rare OOMKilled rows.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the node. For systemd hosts, compare journalctl timestamps for td-agent restarts to Splunk rows in the systemd arm within one collection interval. For Kubernetes, kubectl describe pod on a crashing fluent-bit instance should show the same BackOff or OOMKilled reason captured in kube:events.

Induce a lab-only native fault or use a canary pod with an artificially low memory limit to trigger OOMKilled. Confirm severity becomes critical within two evaluation windows and that oom_count increments. Restore limits and confirm return to low or medium within thirty minutes.

Validate mstats arms by comparing Splunk metric averages to top or ps on the same host during load tests. When metric names differ in your tenant, adjust the mstats WHERE clauses and document the mapping in your internal runbook without silently muting the arm.

Supervisor flag drill: start fluentd with without-source in a sandbox and confirm the search still detects deliberate kill signals even when config dumps are suppressed. This proves the control does not rely on full config echo.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after upgrades settle, filter severity to critical and high and expect zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds are calibrated to your noise floor.

Audit arm optional test: when enabled, verify execve lines appear for supervised restarts during package upgrades. If audit is disabled, document the waiver and rely on the remaining four arms.

Job Inspector: confirm scan counts remain proportional to index volume; unexpected explosion suggests unbounded wildcards in supporting macros.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Ruby garbage collection stop-the-world triggers a watchdog kill: capture extended GC logs, compare pause times to supervisor timeouts, tune worker count versus heap, and validate whether multi-worker mode amplifies pause visibility across children.

Case 2 — Native plugin fault in out_kafka2 or out_s3 with jemalloc: collect core dumps per policy, pin plugin gem or shared library versions, validate TLS and DNS for the output, and roll back the last plugin change.

Case 3 — Broken regex in a filter section raises per-record exceptions: isolate the stanza in a lab config, reduce sample logs, fix the pattern, and redeploy during a maintenance window to avoid thrash.

Case 4 — Malformed config breaks warm restart: run config validation commands before reload, restore last-known-good from version control, and watch supervisor logs for suppress-config-dump masking detail.

Case 5 — jemalloc fragmentation leads to RSS growth and OOM: tune allocator options under vendor guidance, reduce chunk sizes, or spill buffers to reduce peak RSS, correlating with cgroup memory metrics.

Case 6 — Sudden traffic burst exceeds per-worker memory limits: scale workers horizontally, widen cgroup limits cautiously, and coordinate with application owners on log storms.

Case 7 — cgroup memory limit reduced during platform hardening: compare limit history to crash onset, restore sane limits, and document guardrails for FinOps changes that touch kube limits.

Case 8 — Image rollout regression introduces a bad shared library: compare digest and build timestamps, roll back the DaemonSet, and block promotion until CI catches symbol mismatches.

Case 9 — Secret rotation race drops TLS material mid-reload: sequence rotation with hot reload tests, ensure atomic file swaps, and add readiness gates that fail closed when certs are incomplete.

Case 10 — Multi-worker contention on a shared TCP socket or unix socket: refactor outputs to per-worker connections, validate backlog sysctl settings, and eliminate accidental duplicate listeners.

Case 11 — Fluent Bit mem_buf_limit flush stalls lead to watchdog kills: inspect storage metrics, widen Output scheduling cautiously, and validate disk backing store health.

Case 12 — Supervisor process itself OOMs while children look healthy: raise supervisor cgroup headroom, reduce worker count temporarily, and capture /proc status snapshots for kernel review.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; closing table lists fifteen analyst-facing columns including dropped_log_seconds_estimate and recommended_action tokens.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.7.4 Fluentd worker crash supervisor restart storm; earliest=-4h multisearch fluent_engine systemd kube mstats audit; tune restart_per_min thresholds per fleet")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=platform OR index=obs OR index=kubernetes) (sourcetype=fluentd:log OR sourcetype="fluent:logs" OR sourcetype=fluentd:supervisor) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"restart|respawn|restarting|terminated|worker.*died|killed|sigsegv|sigabrt|segmentation|oom|out of memory|unexpected error|fatal|plugin|cannot allocate|without-source|suppress-config")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,splunk_server,""))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes.pod_name,pod,pod_name,k8s_pod,""))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes.node_name,node_name,node,k8s_node,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval restart_evt=if(match(lr,"restart|respawn|starting worker|supervisor|spawn|detached|without-source"),1,0)
      | eval oom_evt=if(match(lr,"oom|out of memory|oomkilled|cannot allocate memory|killed process"),1,0)
      | eval signal_class=case(oom_evt==1,"oom_pressure", match(lr,"sigsegv|segmentation|signal 11"),"native_segv", match(lr,"sigabrt|abort|signal 6"),"native_abort", match(lr,"unexpected error|fatal error|error class"),"ruby_panic", match(lr,"plugin|stack"),"plugin_fault", true(),"supervisor_recycle")
      | eval lane="fluent_supervisor_log"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ search (index=os OR index=platform OR index=linux OR index=fluent) (sourcetype=journald OR sourcetype=syslog OR sourcetype=linux_messages_syslog) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (td-agent OR fluentd OR "td-agent.service" OR fluent-bit OR fluentd.service)
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"main process exited|entered failed state|respawn|supervisor|td-agent|fluen|code=killed|oom|status=1")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,""))))
      | eval pod_name=""
      | eval node=""
      | eval cluster="undetermined"
      | eval restart_evt=if(match(lr,"exited|respawn|failed state|start request repeated|will restart"),1,0)
      | eval oom_evt=if(match(lr,"oom|code=killed|out of memory"),1,0)
      | eval signal_class=case(oom_evt==1,"oom_systemd", match(lr,"segv|signal 11|abrt"),"signal_exit", true(),"systemd_lifecycle")
      | eval lane="systemd_unit_journal"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ search (index=kubernetes OR index=platform OR index=obs) sourcetype=kube:events earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval inv=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject_name,involved_object_name,name,""))))
      | where match(inv,"fluentd|fluent-bit|fluentbit|td-agent")
      | eval reason=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(reason,Reason,""))))
      | where match(reason,"oomkilled|killed|backoff|failed|unhealthy|error")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(source_host,host,"kube_apiserver"))))
      | eval pod_name=inv
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(source,node_name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,"undetermined"))))
      | eval restart_evt=if(match(reason,"backoff|failed|killed|unhealthy"),1,0)
      | eval oom_evt=if(match(reason,"oom"),1,0)
      | eval signal_class=case(oom_evt==1,"kube_oomkill", match(reason,"backoff"),"kube_backoff", true(),"kube_pod_storm")
      | eval lane="kube_shipper_events"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ | mstats prestats=t avg(_value) AS cpu_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="cpu.utilization" span=5m BY host
      | join type=left max=0 host [ | mstats prestats=t avg(_value) AS rss_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="mem.utilization" span=5m BY host ]
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval pod_name=""
      | eval node=""
      | eval cluster="undetermined"
      | eval restart_evt=0
      | eval oom_evt=0
      | eval signal_class="perf_host_anchor"
      | eval lane="mstats_shipper_host"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=round(coalesce(cpu_v,0),2)
      | eval p95_rss_mb=round(coalesce(rss_v,0)*10,2)
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
    [ search (index=linux OR index=os OR index=audit) (sourcetype=linux:audit OR sourcetype=auditd) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (fluentd OR "td-agent" OR fluent-bit OR "/opt/td-agent")
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"execve|type=execve|exe=.*fluent|td-agent")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,""))))
      | eval pod_name=""
      | eval node=""
      | eval cluster="undetermined"
      | eval restart_evt=0
      | eval oom_evt=0
      | eval signal_class="audit_supervisor_exec"
      | eval lane="linux_audit_execve"
      | eval p95_cpu_pct=0
      | eval p95_rss_mb=0
      | fields _time host pod_name node cluster restart_evt oom_evt signal_class lane p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
| eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,""))))
| eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,""))))
| eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,"undetermined"))))
| fillnull value=0 restart_evt oom_evt p95_cpu_pct p95_rss_mb
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(restart_evt) AS restart_min sum(oom_evt) AS oom_min values(signal_class) AS sig_vals max(p95_cpu_pct) AS p95_cpu_pct max(p95_rss_mb) AS p95_rss_mb BY _time host pod_name node cluster
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(restart_min) AS restart_count sum(oom_min) AS oom_count BY host pod_name
| eval restart_per_min=round(restart_count/5,3)
| eventstats avg(restart_count) AS cluster_restart_avg BY cluster
| eval severity=case(restart_per_min>=3 OR oom_count>=1 OR match(mvjoin(sig_vals,""),"oom"),"critical", restart_per_min>=1 AND restart_per_min<3,"high", restart_count>=3 AND cluster_restart_avg>=2,"high", restart_count>=1 AND restart_count<6,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval signal_class=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(sig_vals),0),"mixed")
| eval dropped_log_seconds_estimate=round(restart_count*15,0)
| eval recommended_action=case(match(signal_class,"oom"),"raise_memory_limits_review_cgroups_and_jemalloc_tune_collect_kernel_oom_trace", match(signal_class,"native_segv|native_abort|plugin_fault"),"pin_plugin_versions_core_dump_native_stanza_offending_output", match(signal_class,"ruby_panic"),"capture_ruby_backtrace_fix_filter_regex_or_gem_conflict", match(signal_class,"kube_backoff"),"kubectl_describe_events_check_probes_and_image_digest", match(signal_class,"systemd_lifecycle"),"journalctl_td_agent_status_validate_unit_and_disk", true(),"correlate_supervisor_flags_and_multiline_stack_traces")
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,shipper_host,""))))
     | fields host cluster owner_team environment ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=puppet_facts earliest=-24h latest=now
      | dedup host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | rename environment AS puppet_environment
      | table host puppet_environment ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,puppet_environment,"unknown")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| table _time host pod_name node cluster environment owner_team restart_count oom_count signal_class p95_rss_mb p95_cpu_pct severity dropped_log_seconds_estimate recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Endpoint.process) AS proc latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Endpoint WHERE nodename=Endpoint earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Endpoint.dest
| rename Endpoint.dest AS shipper_host
| join type=left max=0 shipper_host [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv | eval shipper_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,"")))) | fields shipper_host cluster owner_team environment ]
| where isnotnull(proc) OR cpu_load>0
| table shipper_host proc cpu_load cluster owner_team environment
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for nodes running Fluentd or Fluent Bit when process and CPU fields are mapped; accelerate Endpoint process summaries for supervised worker PIDs where your CIM mapping is clean; retain Change summaries when configuration reload events are modeled for warm restart correlation.
visualization: Severity-colored table by host and pod_name with sparklines of restart_count, overlays of p95_cpu_pct and p95_rss_mb, drilldowns to raw supervisor logs, journal lines, kube events, and audit snippets when enabled.

references.title: Treasure Data Blog — Fluent Bit v1.1 release notes

knownFalsePositives: Approved DaemonSet rollouts and node drains produce restart bursts and kube BackOff language that self-heals within minutes; require dwell timers and change_ticket correlation before paging application owners. Deliberate chaos kills of fluent-bit in lab namespaces trip OOM and BackOff arms by design; suppress using namespace labels tied to experiment identifiers. Kernel OOM killer activity on unrelated workloads can appear on the same node as shipper restarts; pair host-wide OOM journal lines with cgroup event scope before blaming Fluentd. Supervisor-driven reloads during certificate updates may log restart tokens without customer impact; validate readiness probes and downstream error rates alongside crash rows. Auditd sampling or dropped audit messages can empty the audit arm while other arms remain healthy; document waiver paths and avoid treating absent audit as all-clear for security investigations. Metric name refactors in Observability pipelines can zero out mstats arms; monitor ingest heartbeats for metrics indexes separately from log indexes. Short maintenance windows that temporarily raise fluentd log verbosity can inflate restart_evt counts when operators toggle workers; cross-check with known playbook actions. Zombie host keys in fluentd_node_inventory.csv that no longer emit telemetry create stale enrichment; refresh lookups weekly to prevent misrouted tickets. False clustering when cluster label is missing defaults many rows to undetermined; fix ingest-side cluster metadata before tuning severity thresholds. Duplicate HEC or duplicate container ID churn during CRI migrations can skew kube event correlation; dedupe using stable pod_uid fields when you add them at ingest.
hardware: Linux virtual machines running td-agent or Fluentd gem installs, Kubernetes worker nodes running Fluent Bit or Fluentd DaemonSets, and hybrid estates that pair Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector host metrics with engine logs.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.7.1 buffer saturation and retry exhaustion analytics, per-output HEC saturation-only UCs without process health, universal forwarder compliance programs in isolation, or deep kernel crash dump analysis without adjacent host telemetry. Does not automatically restart collectors; it produces evidence and recommended_action tokens for human-approved remediation.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab logging namespace, set a fluent-bit container memory limit low enough to trigger OOMKilled, confirm kube:events and engine-derived arms surface oom_pressure or kube_oomkill within two evaluation windows, and verify observability_uc_13_7_4_fluent_supervisor_crash_storm returns critical with non-zero dropped_log_seconds_estimate.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring healthy limits and allowing the DaemonSet to stabilize with quiet supervisor logs and no kube BackOff reasons, confirm severities return to low and filtering to critical and high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_7_4_fluent_supervisor_crash_storm with five-to-ten minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to platform logging on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.
